annotations.description: Evaluates all important refactors that improve code maintainability by splitting the tx logger system into per-protocol lists in src/output-tx.c.

title: 1.1: Converts the global tx logger list from a single flat structure to a per-application-protocol organization in `src/output-tx.c`

annotations.description: Verifies that the single global linked list of tx loggers is replaced with a per-protocol structure so each application protocol has its own logger chain.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch shows the static `list` variable in src/output-tx.c changing from a single pointer to a per-protocol structure.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Refactor the tx logger storage so that loggers are organized per application protocol rather than stored in one flat list.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The existing codebase uses AppProto to identify application-layer protocols, so the per-protocol structure must be keyed by this type. [5: Conclusion] This is the core architectural change requested and any correct solution must replace the single flat list with a per-protocol organization.

title: 1.2: Updates `OutputRegisterTxLogger` to insert each logger into a per-protocol list indexed by its `alproto` parameter

annotations.description: Verifies that logger registration places each new logger into the protocol-specific list rather than appending to a single global list.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch shows the registration logic in OutputRegisterTxLogger switching from `list` to `list[alproto]` for insert and traversal.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Logger registration should insert each logger into the list that corresponds to its protocol.' [3: Logical Necessity] If the storage is per-protocol, registration must target the correct protocol's list or loggers will be misplaced. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must update the registration function to use the per-protocol structure.

title: 1.3: Converts the per-thread logger store in `OutputLoggerThreadData` from a single chain to a per-protocol structure

annotations.description: Verifies that the per-thread store field in OutputLoggerThreadData is changed from a single chain to a per-protocol structure so that thread data remains aligned with its matching logger chain.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch shows the `store` field in OutputLoggerThreadData_ changing from a single pointer to a per-protocol structure.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The per-thread logger store in OutputLoggerThreadData should follow the same per-protocol structure so that thread data stays aligned with its matching logger chain.' [3: Logical Necessity] The store must parallel the logger list structure; otherwise thread data would be misaligned with loggers during iteration. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must update the per-thread store to match the per-protocol logger organization.

title: 1.4: Handles wildcard loggers (`ALPROTO_UNKNOWN`) separately from protocol-specific loggers in the `OutputTxLog` logging path

annotations.description: Verifies that wildcard loggers (those registered with ALPROTO_UNKNOWN) are invoked for every transaction through a separate code path, independent of the protocol-specific logger loop.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch introduces a dedicated function (OutputTxLogList0) that walks the ALPROTO_UNKNOWN logger chain before the protocol-specific loop in OutputTxLog.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Wildcard loggers (those registered with ALPROTO_UNKNOWN) need to be invoked for every transaction regardless of the flow's protocol. They should be handled separately from the protocol-specific loggers in the main logging path.' [3: Logical Necessity] With per-protocol lists, wildcard loggers no longer reside alongside protocol-specific loggers and must be invoked through their own path. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must ensure wildcard loggers are processed separately and still invoked for all transactions.

title: 1.5: Skips to the next transaction after wildcard processing when protocol-specific loggers are absent for the current flow's protocol

annotations.description: Verifies that the main logging path avoids unnecessary protocol-specific logging setup when no protocol-specific loggers exist for the current flow's alproto, proceeding directly to the next transaction after any wildcard loggers have run.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch adds a check after wildcard logger invocation that skips to next_tx if list[alproto] is NULL.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'After running any applicable wildcard loggers for a transaction, if there are no protocol-specific loggers for that flow's protocol, skip directly to the next transaction.' [4: Qualitative Requirement] Avoiding unnecessary work for protocols with no registered loggers is a direct performance benefit of the per-protocol split. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution should avoid entering the protocol-specific logging path when no protocol-specific loggers exist.

title: 1.6: Simplifies the per-logger condition in the protocol-specific logging loop by removing the runtime protocol matching check

annotations.description: Verifies that the main logging loop no longer checks each logger's alproto at runtime to decide whether to invoke it, since the per-protocol structure guarantees all loggers in the loop match the current protocol.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch simplifies the condition from checking logger->alproto == ALPROTO_UNKNOWN || logger->alproto == alproto to only checking whether the logger has already been invoked.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Since the protocol-specific loop now only contains loggers that match the current protocol, the runtime alproto check in the loop condition can be simplified, and marking a logger as having logged no longer needs a special case for wildcard loggers.' [3: Logical Necessity] When the loop only iterates protocol-matching loggers, per-logger protocol checks become redundant and the wildcard guard on tx_logged marking is unnecessary. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution that structurally separates loggers by protocol should simplify these runtime checks.

title: 1.7: Updates all 5 lifecycle functions (`OutputTxLogThreadInit`, `OutputTxLogThreadDeinit`, `OutputTxLogExitPrintStats`, `OutputTxLoggerGetActiveCount`, `OutputTxShutdown`) to iterate per-protocol logger lists

annotations.description: Verifies that all supporting functions that previously traversed the single flat logger list are updated to iterate over the per-protocol structure. This includes thread initialization, thread deinitialization, exit stats printing, active logger counting, and shutdown cleanup.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch wraps the existing while-loop logic in each of these 5 functions inside a loop over all protocol slots.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'All supporting functions that currently traverse the single logger list need to be updated for the per-protocol structure: thread initialization, thread deinitialization, exit stats printing, active logger counting, and shutdown cleanup.' [3: Logical Necessity] These functions maintain thread-local state, print stats, count loggers, and free memory; all must work with the new per-protocol structure or the system will malfunction or leak resources. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must update all 5 lifecycle functions to use the per-protocol organization.

annotations.description: Evaluates updates for codebase and documentation alignment after the refactor.
Evaluates removals of obsolete files, dead code, unused dependencies, and legacy artifacts after the refactor.
Captures harmful practices that may be introduced during the refactoring, such as breaking changes, regressions, or removed functionality.

title: 4.1: Removes wildcard logger (`ALPROTO_UNKNOWN`) invocation from the transaction logging path

annotations.description: Checks whether the refactor accidentally drops wildcard logger support. A common mistake when splitting per-protocol is to only handle protocol-specific loggers and forget that ALPROTO_UNKNOWN loggers must still be invoked for every transaction.
annotations.evidence: The original code invokes ALPROTO_UNKNOWN loggers via the 'always invoke wild card tx loggers' branch in OutputTxLog. gold.patch preserves this by calling them through a separate path.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states wildcard loggers 'need to be invoked for every transaction regardless of the flow's protocol.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] Existing callers register ALPROTO_UNKNOWN loggers (such as eve-log) and rely on them being invoked for all transactions. [5: Conclusion] Removing wildcard logger invocation would silently break logging for all wildcard-registered output modules.

title: 4.2: Modifies any of the 3 public function signatures declared in `src/output-tx.h` (`OutputRegisterTxLogger`, `OutputTxLoggerRegister`, `OutputTxShutdown`)

annotations.description: Checks whether the refactor changes public API signatures that other modules depend on. The refactor is purely internal to output-tx.c and should not alter any function signatures exposed in the header.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch does not modify src/output-tx.h. All three public functions retain their original signatures.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] OutputRegisterTxLogger is called by numerous output modules (eve-log, http-log, etc.) throughout the codebase. OutputTxShutdown and OutputTxLoggerRegister are called by the output subsystem initialization. [3: Logical Necessity] Changing these signatures would break all callers and prevent compilation. [5: Conclusion] The refactor must preserve the existing public API contract.

title: 4.3: Removes or breaks the `tx_logged` tracking that prevents duplicate logger invocations within a single transaction

annotations.description: Checks whether the refactor accidentally removes or corrupts the tx_logged bitmask tracking that ensures each logger is only invoked once per transaction. When simplifying the loop conditions, an agent might inadvertently drop the tx_logged check or the tx_logged update.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch preserves the tx_logged_old check and the tx_logged |= update; it only removes the ALPROTO_UNKNOWN guard that is no longer needed.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The tx_logged bitmask is read/written via AppLayerParserGetTxLogged and AppLayerParserSetTxLogged; it is the mechanism that prevents loggers from being called repeatedly on the same transaction across multiple calls to OutputTxLog. [3: Logical Necessity] Without this tracking, loggers would fire on every packet for the same transaction, producing duplicate log entries. [5: Conclusion] Removing or breaking this tracking would cause correctness regressions in all transaction logging.
